free_text: Institute fur Experimentalphysik.
Graphs.
Tables.
Private communication with data.
Total cross sections and isomeric cross section
ratios for (n,2n) reactions in the energy region
2 MeV Van der Graaff.
2 MeV deuterium beam on a thin, air cooled,
titanium-tritium target. Neutron flux densities of
the order of 10**6 /cm2/s at the sample positions
were produced.
Activation.
Value at 14.1 MeV taken as
689.0+-5mb. Further details of normalisation are
given in EUR-1815E,246,1965.
Stilbene recoil proton spectrometer at that
angle which corresponded to 14.1 MeV neutron energy
was used for flux density measurement.
Neutron flux fluctuations during irradiation were
measured with a second stilbene scintillation counter.
The observed activities were corrected for B-
absorption in the counter window according to the
method proposed by Brovetto et al. , and
for self-absorption according to Schweitzer et al.
Corrections for back-scattering from the sample holder
were applied following the method of Roalsvig et al.
All refs are given in REL-REF.
On the average the deviation of the
experimental points from the smooth trend does not
exceed +- 10 % and thus is of the order of the errors
of the experimental cross sections.
Uncertainty in monitor reaction data
.Table 1 published in EANDC(E)-127,38
Recompiled from NEUDADA.
SD: Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Corrections according last EXFOR rules and Dict.
BIB was updated in all Subents.
REACTION code in Subent 016 was corrected.
.Nb powder.

.Ru powder.
Measurements of beta+ activities were performed
by coincident detection of the annihilation gamma-
quant with a gamma-gamma coincidence spectrometer which
consisted of two 7.6 cm diam x 7.6 cm NaI crystals
placed diametrically opposite with facing front sides.
Measured.
